skill.name: upgrade-stripe
skill.description: Guide for upgrading Stripe API versions and SDKs
skill.summary: Guide for upgrading Stripe API versions and SDKs
skill.author: stripe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, Stripe URLs, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found documentation examples rather than executable skill code. The command examples and network endpoints are relevant to Stripe upgrades and use placeholder credentials. No prompt injection, secret exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Benign external command examples
low_findings.description: The skill includes package-manager and curl examples for updating Stripe libraries and testing API versions. These are documentation examples with placeholder values, not hidden scripts or dynamic command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are visible Markdown examples for npm, pip, and curl. No executable script file, shell interpolation, or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Stripe network references
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to Stripe documentation, Stripe.js, and Stripe API testing examples. These references are expected for a Stripe upgrade guide and do not show credential exfiltration or unrelated endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed URLs are on docs.stripe.com, js.stripe.com, or api.stripe.com. The context is upgrade documentation and test API use with placeholder credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography alerts are false positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines are metadata and Stripe documentation links, not cryptographic algorithm use. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithms being invoked.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain the skill description and a Stripe upgrades documentation link. Semantic review found no cryptographic operation at those locations.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance alert is a false positive
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance line is a section heading for iOS and Android SDKs. No evidence found of host inspection, environment discovery, or system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 129 is documentation text about mobile SDKs. It does not contain commands or instructions that inspect the user's system.

content.value_statement: Stripe API and SDK upgrades can change response shapes, webhook behavior, and client compatibility. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through version review, code updates, and staged testing.
actual_capabilities: Explains Stripe date-based API versioning and breaking change categories.
Shows how to set API versions in dynamic server-side Stripe SDKs.
Describes why strongly typed SDKs should upgrade through SDK versions.
Covers Stripe.js version pairing and mobile SDK version models.
Provides an upgrade checklist for code, webhooks, SDKs, and testing.
Shows how to test a target API version with the Stripe-Version header.
limitations: Does not inspect a repository unless the user provides code or access.
Does not replace Stripe changelog review for product-specific breaking changes.
Uses placeholder Stripe credentials in examples and should not receive live secrets.
Does not guarantee compatibility with future Stripe versions after its published guidance.

use_cases.title: Plan a Stripe API upgrade
use_cases.description: Create a checklist for moving from one Stripe API version to another.

use_cases.title: Update client SDK versions
use_cases.description: Understand which server, browser, and mobile SDKs need version changes.

use_cases.title: Prepare webhook migration tests
use_cases.description: Identify webhook handlers that may need updates for new event structures.

prompt_templates.title: Start an upgrade plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Stripe upgrade from my current API version to 2026-05-27.dahlia. List the review, code, and test steps.

prompt_templates.title: Review SDK versioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Stripe SDK upgrade approach for Node.js, Stripe.js, and mobile clients. Explain which versions control API behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Check webhook readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me audit my webhook handlers for a Stripe API version upgrade. Focus on changed event payloads and unknown event handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create staged rollout guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a staged Stripe API upgrade plan for a production payments system. Include testing with Stripe-Version, rollback considerations, and release sequencing.

output_examples.input: I need to upgrade Stripe from an older API version.
output_examples.output: A phased checklist covering changelog review, SDK updates, API version configuration, webhook validation, and test requests.

output_examples.input: Should I set a custom API version in Java?
output_examples.output: Guidance explaining that strongly typed Stripe SDKs should normally use the API version tied to the SDK release.

output_examples.input: How do I test a new Stripe API version safely?
output_examples.output: A testing plan that uses the Stripe-Version header, webhook fixtures, and staged verification before changing account defaults.

best_practices: Review Stripe changelog entries for every version between the current and target version.
Set API versions explicitly in dynamic SDK integrations when supported.
Test webhook payloads and unknown event handling before production rollout.
anti_patterns: Changing the account default API version before testing code and webhook handlers.
Mixing strongly typed SDK objects with unsupported manual API version overrides.
Sharing live Stripe secret keys in prompts, examples, or logs.

faq.question: Does this skill update my Stripe account automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Account changes still require deliberate action in Stripe tools.

faq.question: Can it help with Stripe.js upgrades?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Stripe.js versioning, versioned script URLs, npm usage, and API version pairing.

faq.question: Does it cover mobile SDKs?
faq.answer: Yes. It summarizes iOS, Android, and React Native versioning models and backend compatibility.

faq.question: Should I provide my Stripe secret key?
faq.answer: No. Use placeholders or test keys only when discussing examples.

faq.question: Can it find breaking changes for my product area?
faq.answer: It can guide changelog review, but product-specific changes should be verified against current Stripe documentation.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production migrations?
faq.answer: Yes, as planning support. Production upgrades still need repository review, test coverage, and operational approval.
